text:
I've always found writing a bit... torturous. I'll start thinking of a subject, do a few researches, and then panic as my mind churns out way more words than I'd ever want. But tonight feels different.

It really shouldn't.

I decide on "The Day Everything Stopped Tickling." 

I take that idea and flesh it out, pulling characters out of my own life. There's Bob, the librarian who collects only rare medical books; Ada, the shy girl who's afraid of dogs but secretly loves furry costumes; and then there's the narrator, who likes to go on long walks listening to poetry anthologies.

...

The plot thickens. Suddenly Ada sees a talking cat on the corner. 

...It wasn't just a cat. It was

Something weird. 

In the middle of the night.

It scratched her arm. 

*...

She screamed.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
